title: 🚀 AsyncKeel: Open-source FastAPI Boilerplate — Public Roadmap & Weekly Progress
content:
## AsyncKeel: Open-source FastAPI Boilerplate — Public Roadmap & Weekly Progress 🚀

Hi devs,

I'm building [AsyncKeel](https://asynckeel.com), an open-source, production-ready FastAPI boilerplate for indie hackers, SaaS builders, and anyone who wants to spin up backends in hours, not weeks.

AsyncKeel ships with:
- Built-in Auth (JWT by default)
- Database layer (SQLModel-ready, SQLite/Postgres support)
- Alembic migrations
- API versioning
- Pagination, rate limiting, and more out of the box

**Why build in public?**  
I've just kicked off our **public roadmap** and will be sharing updates weekly! Transparency helps everyone—both to learn and to build something genuinely useful for the community.

---

## 🔗 Quick links

- 🌍 **Website:** [asynckeel.com](https://asynckeel.com)
- 💻 **GitHub Repo:** [github.com/asynckeel-max/asynckeel](https://github.com/asynckeel-max/asynckeel)
- 🛣️ **Roadmap:** [github.com/users/asynckeel-max/projects/1](https://github.com/users/asynckeel-max/projects/1)
- 🐦 **X/Twitter:** [@AsyncKeel](https://x.com/AsyncKeel)

---

## 🏗 What’s happening this week?

- 🚀 **Built in public** section added to the website
- 🛣️ **Roadmap** launched and publicly viewable
- ⚡ Preparing the first waitlist batch mailing
- 🔜 Upcoming: Demo templates for Postgres & Redis, docs expansion

## How can you help?
- **Star the repo** if you like the idea or want to follow progress.
- **Open an issue** for bugs or feature requests—everything is tracked on the Roadmap!
- **Feedback and suggestions** in comments are very welcome.

---

### PS

If you're also working on an indie dev tool in public, I'd love to connect or share learnings!  
Drop your project link or thoughts below 🚀
